title: North African Harissa & Chickpea Skillet
equipment: 1 large, deep skillet with a lid
ingredients: 800g chicken breast, cut into large chunks
2 cans (400g each) chickpeas, rinsed and drained
Salt, pepper, and a squeeze of lemon juice
instructions: Heat the skillet over medium-high heat. Season the chicken chunks with salt and pepper, then sear them until browned (about 5 minutes).
Lower the heat to medium. Add the harissa paste and stir to coat the chicken.
Pour in the crushed tomatoes and the chickpeas. Stir well.
Remove from heat, stir in the fresh spinach until it wilts, and finish with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice.
content:
# North African Harissa & Chickpea Skillet

Harissa is a North African chili paste packed with roasted peppers, garlic, and warm spices like caraway and coriander. Combined with chickpeas, it creates a deeply flavorful, stew-like base for chicken.

## Equipment
- 1 large, deep skillet with a lid

## Yields
4 servings

## Ingredients
- 800g chicken breast, cut into large chunks
- 2 cans (400g each) chickpeas, rinsed and drained
- 1 can (400g) crushed tomatoes
- 3 tbsp harissa paste
- 150g fresh spinach
- Salt, pepper, and a squeeze of lemon juice

## Instructions
1. Heat the skillet over medium-high heat. Season the chicken chunks with salt and pepper, then sear them until browned (about 5 minutes).
2. Lower the heat to medium. Add the harissa paste and stir to coat the chicken.
3. Pour in the crushed tomatoes and the chickpeas. Stir well.
4. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes.
5. Remove from heat, stir in the fresh spinach until it wilts, and finish with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice.

title: One-Pot Thai Peanut Chicken Curry
ingredients: 1 large bag of frozen stir-fry vegetables (broccoli, carrots, snap peas)
1 tbsp fish sauce (optional, but adds great umami)
instructions: Place the pot over medium heat. Sauté the chicken until lightly browned on the outside.
Add the red curry paste and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the light coconut milk, powdered peanut butter, and fish sauce. Whisk until smooth.
Dump in the frozen vegetables, bring to a gentle bubble, and let it simmer uncovered for 10-15 minutes until the sauce thickens and the chicken is cooked through.
content:
# One-Pot Thai Peanut Chicken Curry

Curries are notoriously high in calories because of full-fat coconut milk and peanut butter. You can hack this for a deficit by using light coconut milk and powdered peanut butter (which removes the fat but keeps the roasted peanut flavor and adds extra protein).

## Equipment
- 1 large pot or Dutch oven

## Yields
4-5 servings

## Ingredients
- 1 kg chicken breast, diced
- 1 can (400ml) light coconut milk
- 3-4 tbsp Thai red curry paste
- 4 tbsp powdered peanut butter (like PB2)
- 1 large bag of frozen stir-fry vegetables (broccoli, carrots, snap peas)
- 1 tbsp fish sauce (optional, but adds great umami)

## Instructions
1. Place the pot over medium heat. Sauté the chicken until lightly browned on the outside.
2. Add the red curry paste and stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
3. Pour in the light coconut milk, powdered peanut butter, and fish sauce. Whisk until smooth.
4. Dump in the frozen vegetables, bring to a gentle bubble, and let it simmer uncovered for 10-15 minutes until the sauce thickens and the chicken is cooked through.

title: Spicy Korean 'Egg Roll in a Bowl'
ingredients: 1 kg lean ground turkey or lean ground pork (5% fat)
1 massive head of cabbage, shredded (or 2 large bags of pre-shredded coleslaw mix)
instructions: Heat the wok over high heat. Add the meat, garlic, and ginger, cooking until the meat is browned.
Stir in the gochujang and soy sauce, mixing until the meat is coated.
Dump all the shredded cabbage into the wok. It will look like too much, but keep tossing it with the meat. It will wilt down in about 5 to 7 minutes.
Remove from heat, stir in the sesame oil, and top heavily with green onions.
content:
# Spicy Korean "Egg Roll in a Bowl"

This is a volume-eating cheat code. Cabbage cooks down significantly but retains a ton of bulk and fiber, making you feel incredibly full for very few calories. The gochujang (Korean chili paste) gives it a deep, savory heat.

## Equipment
- 1 large wok or deep skillet

## Yields
4-5 huge servings

## Ingredients
- 1 kg lean ground turkey or lean ground pork (5% fat)
- 1 massive head of cabbage, shredded (or 2 large bags of pre-shredded coleslaw mix)
- 3 tbsp soy sauce (or tamari)
- 2 tbsp gochujang (adjust for spice preference)
- 1 tbsp sesame oil
- Fresh ginger and garlic (minced)
- Chopped green onions

## Instructions
1. Heat the wok over high heat. Add the meat, garlic, and ginger, cooking until the meat is browned.
2. Stir in the gochujang and soy sauce, mixing until the meat is coated.
3. Dump all the shredded cabbage into the wok. It will look like too much, but keep tossing it with the meat. It will wilt down in about 5 to 7 minutes.
4. Remove from heat, stir in the sesame oil, and top heavily with green onions.
